verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचतयोस्तदद्भ‍ुतं कर्म दावाग्नेर्मोक्षमात्मन: ।गोपा: स्त्रीभ्य: समाचख्यु: प्रलम्बवधमेव च ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācatayos tad adbhutaṁ karmadāvāgner mokṣam ātmanaḥgopāḥ strībhyaḥ samācakhyuḥpralamba-vadham eva ca
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said:To the ladies of Vṛndāvana, the cowherd boys then related in full detail Kṛṣṇa’s and Balarāma’s wonderful activities of delivering them from the forest fire and killing the demon Pralamba.

verses.sanskrit: गोपवृद्धाश्च गोप्यश्च तदुपाकर्ण्य विस्मिता: ।मेनिरे देवप्रवरौ कृष्णरामौ व्रजं गतौ ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: gopa-vṛddhāś ca gopyaś catad upākarṇya vismitāḥmenire deva-pravaraukṛṣṇa-rāmau vrajaṁ gatau
verses.text: The elder cowherd men and ladies were amazed to hear this account, and they concluded that Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma must be exalted demigods who had appeared in Vṛndāvana.

verses.sanskrit: तत: प्रावर्तत प्रावृट् सर्वसत्त्वसमुद्भ‍वा ।विद्योतमानपरिधिर्विस्फूर्जितनभस्तला ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ prāvartata prāvṛṭsarva-sattva-samudbhavāvidyotamāna-paridhirvisphūrjita-nabhas-talā
verses.text: Then the rainy season began, giving life and sustenance to all living beings. The sky began to rumble with thunder, and lightning flashed on the horizon.

verses.sanskrit: सान्द्रनीलाम्बुदैर्व्योम सविद्युत्स्तनयित्नुभि: ।अस्पष्टज्योतिराच्छन्नं ब्रह्मेव सगुणं बभौ ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sāndra-nīlāmbudair vyomasa-vidyut-stanayitnubhiḥaspaṣṭa-jyotir ācchannaṁbrahmeva sa-guṇaṁ babhau
verses.text: The sky was then covered by dense blue clouds accompanied by lightning and thunder. Thus the sky and its natural illumination were covered in the same way that the spirit soul is covered by the three modes of material nature.

verses.sanskrit: अष्टौ मासान् निपीतं यद् भूम्याश्चोदमयं वसु ।स्वगोभिर्मोक्तुमारेभे पर्जन्य: काल आगते ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: aṣṭau māsān nipītaṁ yadbhūmyāś coda-mayaṁ vasusva-gobhir moktum ārebheparjanyaḥ kāla āgate
verses.text: With its rays, the sun had for eight months drunk up the earth’s wealth in the form of water. Now that the proper time had arrived, the sun began releasing this accumulated wealth.

verses.sanskrit: तडिद्वन्तो महामेघाश्चण्डश्वसनवेपिता: ।प्रीणनं जीवनं ह्यस्य मुमुचु: करुणा इव ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: taḍidvanto mahā-meghāścaṇḍa-śvasana-vepitāḥprīṇanaṁ jīvanaṁ hy asyamumucuḥ karuṇā iva
verses.text: Flashing with lightning, great clouds were shaken and swept about by fierce winds. Just like merciful persons, the clouds gave their lives for the pleasure of this world.

verses.sanskrit: तप:कृशा देवमीढा आसीद् वर्षीयसी मही ।यथैव काम्यतपसस्तनु: सम्प्राप्य तत्फलम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tapaḥ-kṛśā deva-mīḍhāāsīd varṣīyasī mahīyathaiva kāmya-tapasastanuḥ samprāpya tat-phalam
verses.text: The earth had been emaciated by the summer heat, but she became fully nourished again when moistened by the god of rain. Thus the earth was like a person whose body has been emaciated by austerities undergone for a material purpose, but who again becomes fully nourished when he achieves the fruit of those austerities.

verses.sanskrit: निशामुखेषु खद्योतास्तमसा भान्ति न ग्रहा: ।यथा पापेन पाषण्डा न हि वेदा: कलौ युगे ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: niśā-mukheṣu khadyotāstamasā bhānti na grahāḥyathā pāpena pāṣaṇḍāna hi vedāḥ kalau yuge
verses.text: In the evening twilight during the rainy season, the darkness allowed the glowworms but not the stars to shine forth, just as in the Age of Kali the predominance of sinful activities allows atheistic doctrines to overshadow the true knowledge of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा पर्जन्यनिनदं मण्डुका: ससृजुर्गिर: ।तूष्णीं शयाना: प्राग् यद्वद्ब्राह्मणा नियमात्यये ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā parjanya-ninadaṁmaṇḍukāḥ sasṛjur giraḥtūṣṇīṁ śayānāḥ prāg yadvadbrāhmaṇā niyamātyaye
verses.text: The frogs, who had all along been lying silent, suddenly began croaking when they heard the rumbling of the rain clouds, in the same way that brāhmaṇa students, who perform their morning duties in silence begin reciting their lessons when called by their teacher.

verses.sanskrit: आसन्नुत्पथगामिन्य: क्षुद्रनद्योऽनुशुष्यती: ।पुंसो यथास्वतन्त्रस्य देहद्रविणसम्पद: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: āsann utpatha-gāminyaḥkṣudra-nadyo ’nuśuṣyatīḥpuṁso yathāsvatantrasyadeha-draviṇa-sampadaḥ
verses.text: With the advent of the rainy season, the insignificant streams, which had become dry, began to swell and then strayed from their proper courses, like the body, property and money of a man controlled by the urges of his senses.

verses.sanskrit: हरिता हरिभि: शष्पैरिन्द्रगोपैश्च लोहिता ।उच्छिलीन्ध्रकृतच्छाया नृणां श्रीरिव भूरभूत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: haritā haribhiḥ śaṣpairindragopaiś ca lohitāucchilīndhra-kṛta-cchāyānṛṇāṁ śrīr iva bhūr abhūt
verses.text: The newly grown grass made the earth emerald green, the indragopa insects added a reddish hue, and white mushrooms added further color and circles of shade. Thus the earth appeared like a person who has suddenly become rich.

verses.sanskrit: क्षेत्राणि शष्यसम्पद्भ‍ि: कर्षकाणां मुदं ददु: ।मानिनामनुतापं वै दैवाधीनमजानताम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣetrāṇi śaṣya-sampadbhiḥkarṣakāṇāṁ mudaṁ daduḥmāninām anutāpaṁ vaidaivādhīnam ajānatām
verses.text: With their wealth of grains, the fields gave joy to the farmers. But those fields created remorse in the hearts of those who were too proud to engage in farming and who failed to understand how everything is under the control of the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: जलस्थलौकस: सर्वे नववारिनिषेवया ।अबिभ्रन् रुचिरं रूपं यथा हरिनिषेवया ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: jala-sthalaukasaḥ sarvenava-vāri-niṣevayāabibhran ruciraṁ rūpaṁyathā hari-niṣevayā
verses.text: As all creatures of the land and water took advantage of the newly fallen rainwater, their forms became attractive and pleasing, just as a devotee becomes beautiful by engaging in the service of the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: सरिद्भ‍ि: सङ्गत: सिन्धुश्चुक्षोभ श्वसनोर्मिमान् ।अपक्‍वयोगिनश्चित्तं कामाक्तं गुणयुग् यथा ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: saridbhiḥ saṅgataḥ sindhuścukṣobha śvasanormimānapakva-yoginaś cittaṁkāmāktaṁ guṇa-yug yathā
verses.text: Where the rivers joined the ocean it became agitated, its waves blown about by the wind, just as the mind of an immature yogī becomes agitated because he is still tainted by lust and attached to the objects of sense gratification.

verses.sanskrit: गिरयो वर्षधाराभिर्हन्यमाना न विव्यथु: ।अभिभूयमाना व्यसनैर्यथाधोक्षजचेतस: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: girayo varṣa-dhārābhirhanyamānā na vivyathuḥabhibhūyamānā vyasanairyathādhokṣaja-cetasaḥ
verses.text: Just as devotees whose minds are absorbed in the Personality of Godhead remain peaceful even when attacked by all sorts of dangers, the mountains in the rainy season were not at all disturbed by the repeated striking of the rain-bearing clouds.

verses.sanskrit: मार्गा बभूवु: सन्दिग्धस्तृणैश्छन्ना ह्यसंस्कृता: ।नाभ्यस्यमाना: श्रुतयो द्विजै: कालेन चाहता: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: mārgā babhūvuḥ sandigdhāstṛṇaiś channā hy asaṁskṛtāḥnābhyasyamānāḥ śrutayodvijaiḥ kālena cāhatāḥ
verses.text: During the rainy season the roads, not being cleansed, became covered with grass and debris and were thus difficult to make out. These roads were like religious scriptures that brāhmaṇas no longer study and that thus become corrupted and covered over with the passage of time.

verses.sanskrit: लोकबन्धुषु मेघेषु विद्युतश्चलसौहृदा: ।स्थैर्यं न चक्रु: कामिन्य: पुरुषेषु गुणिष्विव ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: loka-bandhuṣu megheṣuvidyutaś cala-sauhṛdāḥsthairyaṁ na cakruḥ kāminyaḥpuruṣeṣu guṇiṣv iva
verses.text: Though the clouds are the well-wishing friends of all living beings, the lightning, fickle in its affinities, moved from one group of clouds to another, like lusty women unfaithful even to virtuous men.

verses.sanskrit: धनुर्वियति माहेन्द्रं निर्गुणं च गुणिन्यभात् ।व्यक्ते गुणव्यतिकरेऽगुणवान् पुरुषो यथा ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhanur viyati māhendraṁnirguṇaṁ ca guṇiny abhātvyakte guṇa-vyatikare’guṇavān puruṣo yathā
verses.text: When the curved bow of Indra [the rainbow] appeared in the sky, which had the quality of thundering sound, it was unlike ordinary bows because it did not rest upon a string. Similarly, when the Supreme Lord appears in this world, which is the interaction of the material qualities, He is unlike ordinary persons because He remains free from all material qualities and independent of all material conditions.

verses.sanskrit: न रराजोडुपश्छन्न: स्वज्योत्स्‍नाराजितैर्घनै: ।अहंमत्या भासितया स्वभासा पुरुषो यथा ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: na rarājoḍupaś channaḥsva-jyotsnā-rājitair ghanaiḥahaṁ-matyā bhāsitayāsva-bhāsā puruṣo yathā
verses.text: During the rainy season the moon was prevented from appearing directly by the covering of the clouds, which were themselves illumined by the moon’s rays. Similarly, the living being in material existence is prevented from appearing directly by the covering of the false ego, which is itself illumined by the consciousness of the pure soul.

verses.sanskrit: मेघागमोत्सवा हृष्टा: प्रत्यनन्दञ्छिखण्डिन: ।गृहेषु तप्तनिर्विण्णा यथाच्युतजनागमे ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: meghāgamotsavā hṛṣṭāḥpratyanandañ chikhaṇḍinaḥgṛheṣu tapta-nirviṇṇāyathācyuta-janāgame
verses.text: The peacocks became festive and cried out a joyful greeting when they saw the clouds arrive, just as people distressed in household life feel pleasure when the pure devotees of the infallible Supreme Lord visit them.

verses.sanskrit: पीत्वाप: पादपा: पद्भ‍िरासन्नानात्ममूर्तय: ।प्राक् क्षामास्तपसा श्रान्ता यथा कामानुसेवया ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: pītvāpaḥ pādapāḥ padbhirāsan nānātma-mūrtayaḥprāk kṣāmās tapasā śrāntāyathā kāmānusevayā
verses.text: The trees had grown thin and dry, but after they drank the newly fallen rainwater through their feet, their various bodily features blossomed. Similarly, one whose body has grown thin and weak from austerity again exhibits his healthy bodily features upon enjoying the material objects gained through that austerity.

verses.sanskrit: सर:स्वशान्तरोध:सु न्यूषुरङ्गापि सारसा: ।गृहेष्वशान्तकृत्येषु ग्राम्या इव दुराशया: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: saraḥsv aśānta-rodhaḥsunyūṣur aṅgāpi sārasāḥgṛheṣv aśānta-kṛtyeṣugrāmyā iva durāśayāḥ
verses.text: The cranes continued dwelling on the shores of the lakes, although the shores were agitated during the rainy season, just as materialistic persons with contaminated minds always remain at home, despite the many disturbances there.

verses.sanskrit: जलौघैर्निरभिद्यन्त सेतवो वर्षतीश्वरे ।पाषण्डिनामसद्वादैर्वेदमार्गा: कलौ यथा ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: jalaughair nirabhidyantasetavo varṣatīśvarepāṣaṇḍinām asad-vādairveda-mārgāḥ kalau yathā
verses.text: When Indra sent forth his rains, the floodwaters broke through the irrigation dikes in the agricultural fields, just as in the Kali-yuga the atheists’ false theories break down the boundaries of Vedic injunctions.

verses.sanskrit: व्यमुञ्चन् वायुभिर्नुन्ना भूतेभ्यश्चामृतं घना: ।यथाशिषो विश्पतय: काले काले द्विजेरिता: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyamuñcan vāyubhir nunnābhūtebhyaś cāmṛtaṁ ghanāḥyathāśiṣo viś-patayaḥkāle kāle dvijeritāḥ
verses.text: The clouds, impelled by the winds, released their nectarean water for the benefit of all living beings, just as kings, instructed by their brāhmaṇa priests, dispense charity to the citizens.

verses.sanskrit: एवं वनं तद् वर्षिष्ठं पक्‍वखर्जुरजम्बुमत् ।गोगोपालैर्वृतो रन्तुं सबल: प्राविशद्धरि: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ vanaṁ tad varṣiṣṭhaṁpakva-kharjura-jambumatgo-gopālair vṛto rantuṁsa-balaḥ prāviśad dhariḥ
verses.text: When the Vṛndāvana forest had thus become resplendent, filled with ripe dates and jambu fruits, Lord Kṛṣṇa, surrounded by His cows and cowherd boyfriends and accompanied by Śrī Balarāma, entered that forest to enjoy.

verses.sanskrit: धेनवो मन्दगामिन्य ऊधोभारेण भूयसा ।ययुर्भगवताहूता द्रुतं प्रीत्या स्‍नुतस्तना: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhenavo manda-gāminyaūdho-bhāreṇa bhūyasāyayur bhagavatāhūtādrutaṁ prītyā snuta-stanāḥ
verses.text: The cows had to move slowly because of their weighty milk bags, but they quickly ran to the Supreme Personality of Godhead as soon as He called them, their affection for Him causing their udders to become wet.

verses.sanskrit: वनौकस: प्रमुदिता वनराजीर्मधुच्युत: ।जलधारा गिरेर्नादादासन्ना दद‍ृशे गुहा: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: vanaukasaḥ pramuditāvana-rājīr madhu-cyutaḥjala-dhārā girer nādādāsannā dadṛśe guhāḥ
verses.text: The Lord saw the joyful aborigine girls of the forest, the trees dripping sweet sap, and the mountain waterfalls, whose resounding indicated that there were caves nearby.

verses.sanskrit: क्‍वचिद् वनस्पतिक्रोडे गुहायां चाभिवर्षति ।निर्विश्य भगवान् रेमे कन्दमूलफलाशन: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacid vanaspati-kroḍeguhāyāṁ cābhivarṣatinirviśya bhagavān remekanda-mūla-phalāśanaḥ
verses.text: When it rained, the Lord would sometimes enter a cave or the hollow of a tree to play and to eat roots and fruits.

verses.sanskrit: दध्योदनं समानीतं शिलायां सलिलान्तिके ।सम्भोजनीयैर्बुभुजे गोपै: सङ्कर्षणान्वित: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: dadhy-odanaṁ samānītaṁśilāyāṁ salilāntikesambhojanīyair bubhujegopaiḥ saṅkarṣaṇānvitaḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa would take His meal of boiled rice and yogurt, sent from home, in the company of Lord Saṅkarṣaṇa and the cowherd boys who regularly ate with Him. They would all sit down to eat on a large stone near the water.

verses.sanskrit: शाद्वलोपरि संविश्य चर्वतो मीलितेक्षणान् ।तृप्तान् वृषान् वत्सतरान् गाश्च स्वोधोभरश्रमा: ॥ ३० ॥प्रावृट्‍‍श्रियं च तां वीक्ष्य सर्वकालसुखावहाम् ।भगवान् पूजयां चक्रे आत्मशक्त्युपबृंहिताम् ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: śādvalopari saṁviśyacarvato mīlitekṣaṇāntṛptān vṛṣān vatsatarāngāś ca svodho-bhara-śramāḥ
verses.text: Lord Kṛṣṇa watched the contented bulls, calves and cows sitting on the green grass and grazing with closed eyes, and He saw that the cows were tired from the burden of their heavy milk bags. Thus observing the beauty and opulence of Vṛndāvana’s rainy season, a perennial source of great happiness, the Lord offered all respect to that season, which was expanded from His own internal potency.

verses.sanskrit: एवं निवसतोस्तस्मिन् रामकेशवयोर्व्रजे ।शरत्समभवद् व्यभ्रा स्वच्छाम्ब्वपरुषानिला ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ nivasatos tasminrāma-keśavayor vrajeśarat samabhavad vyabhrāsvacchāmbv-aparuṣānilā
verses.text: While Lord Rāma and Lord Keśava were thus dwelling in Vṛndāvana, the fall season arrived, when the sky is cloudless, the water clear and the wind gentle.

verses.sanskrit: शरदा नीरजोत्पत्त्या नीराणि प्रकृतिं ययु: ।भ्रष्टानामिव चेतांसि पुनर्योगनिषेवया ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śaradā nīrajotpattyānīrāṇi prakṛtiṁ yayuḥbhraṣṭānām iva cetāṁsipunar yoga-niṣevayā
verses.text: The autumn season, which regenerated the lotus flowers, also restored the various bodies of water to their original purity, just as the process of devotional service purifies the minds of the fallen yogīs when they return to it.

verses.sanskrit: व्योम्नोऽब्भ्रं भूतशाबल्यं भुव: पङ्कमपां मलम् ।शरज्जहाराश्रमिणां कृष्णे भक्तिर्यथाशुभम् ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyomno ’bbhraṁ bhūta-śābalyaṁbhuvaḥ paṅkam apāṁ malamśaraj jahārāśramiṇāṁkṛṣṇe bhaktir yathāśubham
verses.text: Autumn cleared the sky of clouds, let the animals get out of their crowded living conditions, cleaned the earth of its covering of mud, and purified the water of contamination, in the same way that loving service rendered to Lord Kṛṣṇa frees the members of the four spiritual orders from their respective troubles.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वस्वं जलदा हित्वा विरेजु: शुभ्रवर्चस: ।यथा त्यक्तैषणा: शान्ता मुनयो मुक्तकिल्बिषा: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sarva-svaṁ jaladā hitvāvirejuḥ śubhra-varcasaḥyathā tyaktaiṣaṇāḥ śāntāmunayo mukta-kilbiṣāḥ
verses.text: The clouds, having given up all they possessed, shone forth with purified effulgence, just like peaceful sages who have given up all material desires and are thus free of all sinful propensities.

verses.sanskrit: गिरयो मुमुचुस्तोयं क्‍वचिन्न मुमुचु: शिवम् ।यथा ज्ञानामृतं काले ज्ञानिनो ददते न वा ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: girayo mumucus toyaṁkvacin na mumucuḥ śivamyathā jñānāmṛtaṁ kālejñānino dadate na vā
verses.text: During this season the mountains sometimes released their pure water and sometimes did not, just as experts in transcendental science sometimes give the nectar of transcendental knowledge and sometimes do not.

verses.sanskrit: नैवाविदन् क्षीयमाणं जलं गाधजलेचरा: ।यथायुरन्वहं क्षय्यं नरा मूढा: कुटुम्बिन: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: naivāvidan kṣīyamāṇaṁjalaṁ gādha-jale-carāḥyathāyur anv-ahaṁ kṣayyaṁnarā mūḍhāḥ kuṭumbinaḥ
verses.text: The fish swimming in the increasingly shallow water did not at all understand that the water was diminishing, just as foolish family men cannot see how the time they have left to live is diminishing with every passing day.

verses.sanskrit: गाधवारिचरास्तापमविन्दञ्छरदर्कजम् ।यथा दरिद्र: कृपण: कुटुम्ब्यविजितेन्द्रिय: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: gādha-vāri-carās tāpamavindañ charad-arka-jamyathā daridraḥ kṛpaṇaḥkuṭumby avijitendriyaḥ
verses.text: Just as a miserly, poverty-stricken person overly absorbed in family life suffers because he cannot control his senses, the fish swimming in the shallow water had to suffer the heat of the autumn sun.

verses.sanskrit: शनै: शनैर्जहु: पङ्कं स्थलान्यामं च वीरुध: ।यथाहंममतां धीरा: शरीरादिष्वनात्मसु ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śanaiḥ śanair jahuḥ paṅkaṁsthalāny āmaṁ ca vīrudhaḥyathāhaṁ-mamatāṁ dhīrāḥśarīrādiṣv anātmasu
verses.text: Gradually the different areas of land gave up their muddy condition and the plants grew past their unripe stage, in the same way that sober sages give up egotism and possessiveness. These are based on things different from the real self, namely, the material body and its by-products.

verses.sanskrit: निश्चलाम्बुरभूत्तूष्णीं समुद्र: शरदागमे ।आत्मन्युपरते सम्यङ्‍मुनिर्व्युपरतागम: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: niścalāmbur abhūt tūṣṇīṁsamudraḥ śarad-āgameātmany uparate samyaṅmunir vyuparatāgamaḥ
verses.text: With the arrival of autumn, the ocean and the lakes became silent, their water still, just like a sage who has desisted from all material activities and given up his recitation of Vedic mantras.

verses.sanskrit: केदारेभ्यस्त्वपोऽगृह्णन् कर्षका द‍ृढसेतुभि: ।यथा प्राणै: स्रवज्ज्ञानं तन्निरोधेन योगिन: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: kedārebhyas tv apo ’gṛhṇankarṣakā dṛḍha-setubhiḥyathā prāṇaiḥ sravaj jñānaṁtan-nirodhena yoginaḥ
verses.text: In the same way that the practitioners of yoga bring their senses under strict control to check their consciousness from flowing out through the agitated senses, the farmers erected strong mud banks to keep the water within their rice fields from draining out.

verses.sanskrit: शरदर्कांशुजांस्तापान् भूतानामुडुपोऽहरत् ।देहाभिमानजं बोधो मुकुन्दो व्रजयोषिताम् ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: śarad-arkāṁśu-jāṁs tāpānbhūtānām uḍupo ’haratdehābhimāna-jaṁ bodhomukundo vraja-yoṣitām
verses.text: The autumn moon relieved all creatures of the suffering caused by the sun’s rays, just as wisdom relieves a person of the misery caused by his identifying with his material body and as Lord Mukunda relieves Vṛndāvana’s ladies of the distress caused by their separation from Him.

verses.sanskrit: खमशोभत निर्मेघं शरद्विमलतारकम् ।सत्त्वयुक्तं यथा चित्तं शब्दब्रह्मार्थदर्शनम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: kham aśobhata nirmeghaṁśarad-vimala-tārakamsattva-yuktaṁ yathā cittaṁśabda-brahmārtha-darśanam
verses.text: Free of clouds and filled with clearly visible stars, the autumn sky shone brilliantly, just like the spiritual consciousness of one who has directly experienced the purport of the Vedic scriptures.

verses.sanskrit: अखण्डमण्डलो व्योम्नि रराजोडुगणै: शशी ।यथा यदुपति: कृष्णो वृष्णिचक्रावृतो भुवि ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: akhaṇḍa-maṇḍalo vyomnirarājoḍu-gaṇaiḥ śaśīyathā yadu-patiḥ kṛṣṇovṛṣṇi-cakrāvṛto bhuvi
verses.text: The full moon shone in the sky, surrounded by stars, just as Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the Yadu dynasty, shone brilliantly on the earth, surrounded by all the Vṛṣṇis.

verses.sanskrit: आश्लिष्य समशीतोष्णं प्रसूनवनमारुतम् ।जनास्तापं जहुर्गोप्यो न कृष्णहृतचेतस: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: āśliṣya sama-śītoṣṇaṁprasūna-vana-mārutamjanās tāpaṁ jahur gopyona kṛṣṇa-hṛta-cetasaḥ
verses.text: Except for the gopīs, whose hearts had been stolen by Kṛṣṇa, the people could forget their suffering by embracing the wind coming from the flower-filled forest. This wind was neither hot nor cold.

verses.sanskrit: गावो मृगा: खगा नार्य: पुष्पिण्य: शरदाभवन् ।अन्वीयमाना: स्ववृषै: फलैरीशक्रिया इव ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: gāvo mṛgāḥ khagā nāryaḥpuṣpiṇyaḥ śaradābhavananvīyamānāḥ sva-vṛṣaiḥphalair īśa-kriyā iva
verses.text: By the influence of the autumn season, all the cows, doe, women and female birds became fertile and were followed by their respective mates in search of sexual enjoyment, just as activities performed for the service of the Supreme Lord are automatically followed by all beneficial results.

verses.sanskrit: उदहृष्यन् वारिजानि सूर्योत्थाने कुमुद् विना ।राज्ञा तु निर्भया लोका यथा दस्यून् विना नृप ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: udahṛṣyan vārijānisūryotthāne kumud vinārājñā tu nirbhayā lokāyathā dasyūn vinā nṛpa
verses.text: O King Parīkṣit, when the autumn sun rose, all the lotus flowers blossomed happily, except the night-blooming kumut, just as in the presence of a strong ruler everyone becomes fearless, except the thieves.

verses.sanskrit: पुरग्रामेष्वाग्रयणैरिन्द्रियैश्च महोत्सवै: ।बभौ भू: पक्‍वशष्याढ्या कलाभ्यां नितरां हरे: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: pura-grāmeṣv āgrayaṇairindriyaiś ca mahotsavaiḥbabhau bhūḥ pakva-śaṣyāḍhyākalābhyāṁ nitarāṁ hareḥ
verses.text: In all the towns and villages people held great festivals, performing the Vedic fire sacrifice for honoring and tasting the first grains of the new harvest, along with similar celebrations that followed local custom and tradition. Thus the earth, rich with newly grown grain and especially beautified by the presence of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, shone beautifully as an expansion of the Supreme Lord.

verses.sanskrit: वणिङ्‍मुनिनृपस्‍नाता निर्गम्यार्थान् प्रपेदिरे ।वर्षरुद्धा यथा सिद्धा: स्वपिण्डान् काल आगते ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaṇiṅ-muni-nṛpa-snātānirgamyārthān prapedirevarṣa-ruddhā yathā siddhāḥsva-piṇḍān kāla āgate
verses.text: The merchants, sages, kings and brahmacārī students, kept in by the rain, were at last free to go out and attain their desired objects, just as those who achieve perfection in this life can, when the proper time comes, leave the material body and attain their respective forms.
